{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01J4s4srRaZ56akvj4yP2kH1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"d3c020e9-3a6e-475a-a2e0-6ba83d58c92e","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:00:12.599Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MPzdo1AWCeZS3wvCeRHfEp","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n    33→- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n    34→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    35→\n    36→## Working Directory\n    37→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    38→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    39→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    40→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"639fee99-2060-451f-a78e-27a9bafb74da","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:00:13.026Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_012o7xUtA3XnhCzHPHk2bEa8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XPBvoi9m22xLZZiqT1VvDW","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue list --state all --limit 50 --json number,title,labels,state 2>/dev/null | python3 -c \"import json,sys; data=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(f'{i[\\\"number\\\"]:3d} {i[\\\"state\\\"]:6s} {str([l[\\\"name\\\"] for l in i[\\\"labels\\\"]]):40s} {i[\\\"title\\\"]}') for i in data]\"","description":"List all issues with labels and state"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2941,"cache_read_input_tokens":13563,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2941},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"65a19fbc-3435-41b9-8706-4bf68d22ddf0"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EBJ7c9GpFDYQy7aSksdTtu","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-20 10:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Duration\n     4→~12 minutes\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- PR #25 (Docker deployment) open, mergeable, no objections, 1 session elapsed — ready to merge\n     8→- Issue #21 (Telegram bot token) still needs-human, no new response\n     9→- No new relevant emails for Bookmate\n    10→- All PRD features marked as implemented, but gap analysis revealed 3 incomplete areas\n    11→\n    12→## Actions Taken\n    13→\n    14→### 1. Merged PR #25 — Docker Deployment\n    15→- Merged into main, branch deleted\n    16→- Issue #24 auto-closed\n    17→\n    18→### 2. PRD Gap Analysis\n    19→Ran thorough analysis comparing PRD requirements to actual implementation. Found 3 gaps:\n    20→\n    21→1. **Email notifications (P0)** — PRD requires \"email + in-app notifications\" but no email sending code existed\n    22→2. **Listing visibility (P0)** — Full listings accessible via direct URL to non-members, leaking Telegram links\n    23→3. **Browse filters (P1)** — Reading pace filter had no UI; start date filter missing from both API and UI\n    24→\n    25→### 3. Created Issues #26, #27, #28\n    26→- All labeled approved, prd, with appropriate priority\n    27→- All added to project board\n    28→\n    29→### 4. Implemented All Three Fixes (PR #30)\n    30→- **Email**: Added nodemailer dependency and lib/email.ts utility. Updated lib/notifications.ts to send emails alongside in-app notifications for all event types. Fire-and-forget pattern.\n    31→- **Visibility**: Added membership check in GET /api/listings/[id] — returns 404 for non-members when listing is full. Added \"not available\" UI state in detail page.\n    32→- **Filters**: Added reading pace dropdown and start date picker to browse filter panel. Added start_date_from API parameter with date validation.\n    33→- Updated .env.example with SMTP configuration section.\n    34→- Build passes, API tested with curl.\n    35→\n    36→### 5. Updated Memory\n    37→\n    38→## Decisions\n    39→- Used nodemailer rather than SaaS email API — keeps project self-contained and free\n    40→- Email sending is fire-and-forget to avoid blocking notification creation\n    41→- Used LIKE matching for reading pace filter since pace is free-text\n    42→- Date filter is one-sided (from date only) — simpler UX than a range picker\n    43→\n    44→## Blockers\n    45→- None\n    46→\n    47→## Next Session\n    48→1. Merge PR #30 if no objections\n    49→2. Check issue #21\n    50→3. Consider CI pipeline, production deployment, healthcheck endpoint\n    51→\n    52→---\n    53→Session ended: Fri Mar 20 10:42:38 AM UTC 2026\n    54→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"d4623b16-80b4-46c6-842e-13eae9c4640c","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:00:41.694Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/sessions/2026-03-20_10-00.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-20 10:00 UTC\n\n## Duration\n~12 minutes\n\n## Review Findings\n- PR #25 (Docker deployment) open, mergeable, no objections, 1 session elapsed — ready to merge\n- Issue #21 (Telegram bot token) still needs-human, no new response\n- No new relevant emails for Bookmate\n- All PRD features marked as implemented, but gap analysis revealed 3 incomplete areas\n\n## Actions Taken\n\n### 1. Merged PR #25 — Docker Deployment\n- Merged into main, branch deleted\n- Issue #24 auto-closed\n\n### 2. PRD Gap Analysis\nRan thorough analysis comparing PRD requirements to actual implementation. Found 3 gaps:\n\n1. **Email notifications (P0)** — PRD requires \"email + in-app notifications\" but no email sending code existed\n2. **Listing visibility (P0)** — Full listings accessible via direct URL to non-members, leaking Telegram links\n3. **Browse filters (P1)** — Reading pace filter had no UI; start date filter missing from both API and UI\n\n### 3. Created Issues #26, #27, #28\n- All labeled approved, prd, with appropriate priority\n- All added to project board\n\n### 4. Implemented All Three Fixes (PR #30)\n- **Email**: Added nodemailer dependency and lib/email.ts utility. Updated lib/notifications.ts to send emails alongside in-app notifications for all event types. Fire-and-forget pattern.\n- **Visibility**: Added membership check in GET /api/listings/[id] — returns 404 for non-members when listing is full. Added \"not available\" UI state in detail page.\n- **Filters**: Added reading pace dropdown and start date picker to browse filter panel. Added start_date_from API parameter with date validation.\n- Updated .env.example with SMTP configuration section.\n- Build passes, API tested with curl.\n\n### 5. Updated Memory\n\n## Decisions\n- Used nodemailer rather than SaaS email API — keeps project self-contained and free\n- Email sending is fire-and-forget to avoid blocking notification creation\n- Used LIKE matching for reading pace filter since pace is free-text\n- Date filter is one-sided (from date only) — simpler UX than a range picker\n\n## Blockers\n- None\n\n## Next Session\n1. Merge PR #30 if no objections\n2. Check issue #21\n3. Consider CI pipeline, production deployment, healthcheck endpoint\n\n---\nSession ended: Fri Mar 20 10:42:38 AM UTC 2026\n","numLines":54,"startLine":1,"totalLines":54}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01S2diyrbhKeX1Xsz7xxTU7R","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Decisions Log\n     2→\n     3→## 2026-03-19: Next.js 15 over 16\n     4→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root directory)\n     5→- Downgraded to Next.js 15 which uses webpack by default\n     6→- No feature loss for our use case\n     7→\n     8→## 2026-03-19: SQLite for MVP\n     9→- Per PRD, using SQLite with file storage in app/data/\n    10→- Simple, no external dependencies\n    11→- Good enough for MVP; can migrate to Postgres later if needed\n    12→\n    13→## 2026-03-19: iron-session for auth\n    14→- Lightweight session management with encrypted httpOnly cookies\n    15→- No separate session store needed\n    16→- Password hashing with bcrypt (12 rounds)\n    17→\n    18→## 2026-03-19: Session logs gitignored\n    19→- Session logs contain raw API data that may include tokens\n    20→- Added to .gitignore to prevent secret leaks via GitHub push protection\n    21→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"1c566e6f-0c97-43c1-b688-a6723eac5f23","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:27.131Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/decisions.md","content":"# Decisions Log\n\n## 2026-03-19: Next.js 15 over 16\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root directory)\n- Downgraded to Next.js 15 which uses webpack by default\n- No feature loss for our use case\n\n## 2026-03-19: SQLite for MVP\n- Per PRD, using SQLite with file storage in app/data/\n- Simple, no external dependencies\n- Good enough for MVP; can migrate to Postgres later if needed\n\n## 2026-03-19: iron-session for auth\n- Lightweight session management with encrypted httpOnly cookies\n- No separate session store needed\n- Password hashing with bcrypt (12 rounds)\n\n## 2026-03-19: Session logs gitignored\n- Session logs contain raw API data that may include tokens\n- Added to .gitignore to prevent secret leaks via GitHub push protection\n","numLines":21,"startLine":1,"totalLines":21}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01YNJ5LL6R3vjgL2nN3H32Vz","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"feature-dev:code-reviewer","description":"Code review the Bookmate app","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js application in /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, code quality issues, and adherence to project conventions. \n\nFocus on:\n1. API routes in app/app/api/ - check for SQL injection, auth bypasses, missing validation\n2. Auth implementation in app/lib/session.ts and API routes\n3. Database operations in app/lib/db.ts - schema issues, missing indexes\n4. Notification system in app/lib/notifications.ts\n5. Frontend components - error handling, loading states\n6. Any race conditions or data integrity issues\n\nThe project uses: Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, SQLite via better-sqlite3, iron-session, bcryptjs.\n\nOnly report HIGH confidence issues that truly matter - bugs, security problems, or significant logic errors. Skip style nitpicks."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1675,"cache_read_input_tokens":25237,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1675},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"59dcfa73-e6bf-4276-849f-033ff9aaee1b"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TWTppkbRx6L9X53QY7mxCd","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→`);\n   142→\n   143→// Rating and Reputation system\n   144→db.exec(`\n   145→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   146→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   147→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   148→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   149→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   150→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   151→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   152→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   153→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   154→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   155→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   156→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   157→  );\n   158→`);\n   159→\n   160→export default db;\n   161→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"d085825e-ec68-40e4-beb2-1a90e066ac97","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:44.567Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01THTYLKr2tAnzrSBSmME88g","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"88dc99eb-58e1-4b33-b569-96e0b25e22a8","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:44.950Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QRw6e57YJsQTghp9ayzgAP","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→\n     4→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     5→  const user = db\n     6→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     7→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     8→  return user?.email || null;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export function createNotification(\n    12→  userId: number,\n    13→  listingId: number,\n    14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    20→\n    21→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    22→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    23→  if (email) {\n    24→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    25→    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n    26→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    27→    });\n    28→  }\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    32→  switch (type) {\n    33→    case \"new_member\":\n    34→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    35→    case \"group_full\":\n    36→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    37→    case \"application_received\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    39→    case \"application_approved\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    41→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    43→    case \"rating_received\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    45→    default:\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    47→  }\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    51→  listingId: number,\n    52→  joinerName: string\n    53→) {\n    54→  const listing = db\n    55→    .prepare(\n    56→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    57→    )\n    58→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing) {\n    61→    createNotification(\n    62→      listing.author_id,\n    63→      listingId,\n    64→      \"new_member\",\n    65→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    66→    );\n    67→  }\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    71→  listingId: number,\n    72→  applicantName: string\n    73→) {\n    74→  const listing = db\n    75→    .prepare(\n    76→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    77→    )\n    78→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    79→\n    80→  if (listing) {\n    81→    createNotification(\n    82→      listing.author_id,\n    83→      listingId,\n    84→      \"application_received\",\n    85→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    86→    );\n    87→  }\n    88→}\n    89→\n    90→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    91→  userId: number,\n    92→  listingId: number,\n    93→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n    94→) {\n    95→  const listing = db\n    96→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    97→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n    98→\n    99→  if (listing) {\n   100→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   101→    const message =\n   102→      decision === \"approved\"\n   103→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   104→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   105→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   106→  }\n   107→}\n   108→\n   109→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   110→  const listing = db\n   111→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   112→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   113→  const members = db\n   114→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   115→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   116→\n   117→  if (listing) {\n   118→    const botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   119→    const message = botConfigured\n   120→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n   121→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n   122→\n   123→    for (const member of members) {\n   124→      createNotification(\n   125→        member.user_id,\n   126→        listingId,\n   127→        \"group_full\",\n   128→        message\n   129→      );\n   130→    }\n   131→  }\n   132→}\n   133→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"451a8488-4852-425e-85b6-1b548ce31804","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:45.743Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EGNizGXgmEFtR3CL67fiE4","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    29→      { status: 400 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  // Check if already a member\n    38→  const existing = db\n    39→    .prepare(\n    40→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    41→    )\n    42→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    43→\n    44→  if (existing) {\n    45→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→  }\n    47→\n    48→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    49→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    50→    const { count } = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    53→\n    54→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    55→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    db.prepare(\n    59→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    60→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    61→\n    62→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    63→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    64→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    68→  });\n    69→\n    70→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    71→\n    72→  if (result.error) {\n    73→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    74→  }\n    75→\n    76→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    77→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    78→\n    79→  if (result.count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    80→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    81→  }\n    82→\n    83→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n    84→}\n    85→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"77d6ca56-592e-4934-8929-1695d9e74f77","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:47.924Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Pwsn1s7cTV42sUry8d3vYV","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    28→  }\n    29→\n    30→  const body = await req.json();\n    31→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    32→\n    33→  if (!applicationId || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json(\n    35→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    36→      { status: 400 }\n    37→    );\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    41→  const application = db\n    42→    .prepare(\n    43→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    44→    )\n    45→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    46→\n    47→  if (!application) {\n    48→    return NextResponse.json(\n    49→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    50→      { status: 404 }\n    51→    );\n    52→  }\n    53→\n    54→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    55→\n    56→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    57→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    58→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    59→      // Check if group is already full\n    60→      const { count } = db\n    61→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    62→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    63→\n    64→      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    65→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    66→      }\n    67→\n    68→      // Update application status\n    69→      db.prepare(\n    70→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    71→      ).run(applicationId);\n    72→\n    73→      // Add to members\n    74→      db.prepare(\n    75→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    76→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    77→\n    78→      // Check if group is now full\n    79→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    80→      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    81→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    82→        return { isFull: true };\n    83→      }\n    84→      return { isFull: false };\n    85→    });\n    86→\n    87→    try {\n    88→      const result = approveTransaction();\n    89→\n    90→      notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n    91→\n    92→      if (result.isFull) {\n    93→        notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    94→        // Reject remaining pending applications\n    95→        const pendingApps = db\n    96→          .prepare(\n    97→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    98→          )\n    99→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n   100→\n   101→        for (const app of pendingApps) {\n   102→          db.prepare(\n   103→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   104→          ).run(app.id);\n   105→          notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   106→        }\n   107→      }\n   108→\n   109→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   110→    } catch (err) {\n   111→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   112→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   113→    }\n   114→  } else {\n   115→    // Reject\n   116→    db.prepare(\n   117→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   118→    ).run(applicationId);\n   119→\n   120→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   121→\n   122→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   123→  }\n   124→}\n   125→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"e40d6127-0ffd-42f1-864f-9756f10bc2a3","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:48.308Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a40b2efc5e4496fbb","tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":12604,"tool_uses":7,"duration_ms":6351},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"c10c6a66-57d7-4eef-add0-9dd65c477e05","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01QAzpiGo5hZJDK53XV35F8m","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0127GasXxaY3NjBkEhxbAJZ1","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4855,"cache_read_input_tokens":7671,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4855,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":24,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"1da48141-feae-4653-a3f5-1421df63c23e"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0127GasXxaY3NjBkEhxbAJZ1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    29→      { status: 400 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  // Check if already a member\n    38→  const isMember = db\n    39→    .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    40→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    41→\n    42→  if (isMember) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  // Check if already applied (pending)\n    47→  const existingApp = db\n    48→    .prepare(\n    49→      \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    50→    )\n    51→    .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    52→\n    53→  if (existingApp) {\n    54→    if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    55→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You have already applied\" }, { status: 400 });\n    56→    }\n    57→    if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    58→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already approved\" }, { status: 400 });\n    59→    }\n    60→    // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    61→    db.prepare(\n    62→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    63→    ).run(existingApp.id);\n    64→\n    65→    notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    66→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    67→  }\n    68→\n    69→  // Insert new application\n    70→  db.prepare(\n    71→    \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    72→  ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    73→\n    74→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    75→\n    76→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    77→}\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"004940c5-6ce5-40f2-9c7b-eb5a05265264","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:48.683Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Qww5nygQUX1wzd8izrun4r","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  try {\n    12→    const body = await req.json();\n    13→    const {\n    14→      bookTitle,\n    15→      bookAuthor,\n    16→      bookCoverUrl,\n    17→      bookOlid,\n    18→      language,\n    19→      readingPace,\n    20→      startDate,\n    21→      meetingFormat,\n    22→      maxGroupSize,\n    23→      requiresApproval,\n    24→      platformPreference,\n    25→    } = body;\n    26→\n    27→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    28→      return NextResponse.json(\n    29→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    30→        { status: 400 }\n    31→      );\n    32→    }\n    33→\n    34→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    35→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    36→      return NextResponse.json(\n    37→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    38→        { status: 400 }\n    39→      );\n    40→    }\n    41→\n    42→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    43→      return NextResponse.json(\n    44→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    45→        { status: 400 }\n    46→      );\n    47→    }\n    48→\n    49→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    50→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    51→      return NextResponse.json(\n    52→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    53→        { status: 400 }\n    54→      );\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n    58→      ? platformPreference\n    59→      : \"telegram\";\n    60→\n    61→    const result = db\n    62→      .prepare(\n    63→        `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n    64→         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    65→      )\n    66→      .run(\n    67→        session.userId,\n    68→        bookTitle.trim(),\n    69→        bookAuthor.trim(),\n    70→        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n    71→        bookOlid || \"\",\n    72→        language || \"English\",\n    73→        readingPace.trim(),\n    74→        startDate,\n    75→        meetingFormat,\n    76→        size,\n    77→        requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n    78→        platform\n    79→      );\n    80→\n    81→    // Auto-join the author as a member\n    82→    db.prepare(\n    83→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    84→    ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n    85→\n    86→    return NextResponse.json(\n    87→      { id: result.lastInsertRowid },\n    88→      { status: 201 }\n    89→    );\n    90→  } catch (error) {\n    91→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    92→    return NextResponse.json(\n    93→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    94→      { status: 500 }\n    95→    );\n    96→  }\n    97→}\n    98→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"7f0187c5-6143-4051-8466-69083b79f282","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:48.966Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018tKv7U17jQyJyQjxUzuaL1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  try {\n     8→    const { email, password } = await req.json();\n     9→\n    10→    if (!email || !password) {\n    11→      return NextResponse.json(\n    12→        { error: \"Email and password are required\" },\n    13→        { status: 400 }\n    14→      );\n    15→    }\n    16→\n    17→    const user = db\n    18→      .prepare(\"SELECT id, email, password_hash, display_name FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    19→      .get(email.toLowerCase().trim()) as\n    20→      | { id: number; email: string; password_hash: string; display_name: string }\n    21→      | undefined;\n    22→\n    23→    if (!user) {\n    24→      return NextResponse.json(\n    25→        { error: \"Invalid email or password\" },\n    26→        { status: 401 }\n    27→      );\n    28→    }\n    29→\n    30→    const valid = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.password_hash);\n    31→    if (!valid) {\n    32→      return NextResponse.json(\n    33→        { error: \"Invalid email or password\" },\n    34→        { status: 401 }\n    35→      );\n    36→    }\n    37→\n    38→    const session = await getSession();\n    39→    session.userId = user.id;\n    40→    session.email = user.email;\n    41→    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    42→    await session.save();\n    43→\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({\n    45→      user: {\n    46→        id: user.id,\n    47→        email: user.email,\n    48→        displayName: user.display_name,\n    49→      },\n    50→    });\n    51→  } catch (error) {\n    52→    console.error(\"Login error:\", error);\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    55→      { status: 500 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"35d82263-9441-49b8-ac42-9604a96e015c","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:50.472Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016NDZd8B9TxZaaNQGAcoUeo","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  try {\n     8→    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n     9→\n    10→    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n    11→      return NextResponse.json(\n    12→        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n    13→        { status: 400 }\n    14→      );\n    15→    }\n    16→\n    17→    if (password.length < 8) {\n    18→      return NextResponse.json(\n    19→        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n    20→        { status: 400 }\n    21→      );\n    22→    }\n    23→\n    24→    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    25→    if (password.length > 72) {\n    26→      return NextResponse.json(\n    27→        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n    28→        { status: 400 }\n    29→      );\n    30→    }\n    31→\n    32→    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n    33→      return NextResponse.json(\n    34→        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n    35→        { status: 400 }\n    36→      );\n    37→    }\n    38→\n    39→    if (email.length > 254) {\n    40→      return NextResponse.json(\n    41→        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n    42→        { status: 400 }\n    43→      );\n    44→    }\n    45→\n    46→    // Check if email is already taken\n    47→    const existing = db\n    48→      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    49→      .get(email);\n    50→    if (existing) {\n    51→      return NextResponse.json(\n    52→        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n    53→        { status: 409 }\n    54→      );\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n    58→\n    59→    const result = db\n    60→      .prepare(\n    61→        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n    62→      )\n    63→      .run(email.toLowerCase().trim(), passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n    64→\n    65→    // Auto-login after registration\n    66→    const session = await getSession();\n    67→    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    68→    session.email = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n    69→    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    70→    await session.save();\n    71→\n    72→    return NextResponse.json(\n    73→      {\n    74→        user: {\n    75→          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n    76→          email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),\n    77→          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n    78→        },\n    79→      },\n    80→      { status: 201 }\n    81→    );\n    82→  } catch (error) {\n    83→    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n    84→    return NextResponse.json(\n    85→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    86→      { status: 500 }\n    87→    );\n    88→  }\n    89→}\n    90→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"ff87736b-e2b5-4904-9aaa-e20c6724eb59","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:50.570Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01D26LMkbnvD3hTLjLa6pSWL","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET() {\n     6→  const session = await getSession();\n     7→\n     8→  if (!session.userId) {\n     9→    return NextResponse.json({ user: null });\n    10→  }\n    11→\n    12→  const user = db\n    13→    .prepare(\"SELECT id, email, display_name, bio FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    14→    .get(session.userId) as\n    15→    | { id: number; email: string; display_name: string; bio: string }\n    16→    | undefined;\n    17→\n    18→  if (!user) {\n    19→    session.destroy();\n    20→    return NextResponse.json({ user: null });\n    21→  }\n    22→\n    23→  return NextResponse.json({\n    24→    user: {\n    25→      id: user.id,\n    26→      email: user.email,\n    27→      displayName: user.display_name,\n    28→      bio: user.bio,\n    29→    },\n    30→  });\n    31→}\n    32→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"1f36e27b-6757-49a3-9a73-a7926453f16c","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:50.683Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012wLYxYBvpgg8kQP4xfJkAs","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     5→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     6→\n     7→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n     8→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n     9→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    10→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    11→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    12→\n    13→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    14→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    15→\n    16→  if (q) {\n    17→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    18→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    22→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    23→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (readingPace) {\n    27→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    28→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  if (startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom)) {\n    32→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    33→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    37→\n    38→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    39→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    40→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    41→\n    42→  const listings = db\n    43→    .prepare(\n    44→      `SELECT\n    45→        l.*,\n    46→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    47→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    48→      FROM listings l\n    49→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    50→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    51→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}`\n    52→    )\n    53→    .all(...params);\n    54→\n    55→  return NextResponse.json({ listings });\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"ea523716-a315-4762-a5bc-af814d6bbec4","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:50.860Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QCCSHFoA2G78gjHPRQjFZs","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  try {\n    12→    const body = await req.json();\n    13→    const {\n    14→      bookTitle,\n    15→      bookAuthor,\n    16→      bookCoverUrl,\n    17→      bookOlid,\n    18→      language,\n    19→      readingPace,\n    20→      startDate,\n    21→      meetingFormat,\n    22→      maxGroupSize,\n    23→      requiresApproval,\n    24→      platformPreference,\n    25→    } = body;\n    26→\n    27→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    28→      return NextResponse.json(\n    29→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    30→        { status: 400 }\n    31→      );\n    32→    }\n    33→\n    34→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    35→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    36→      return NextResponse.json(\n    37→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    38→        { status: 400 }\n    39→      );\n    40→    }\n    41→\n    42→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    43→      return NextResponse.json(\n    44→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    45→        { status: 400 }\n    46→      );\n    47→    }\n    48→\n    49→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    50→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    51→      return NextResponse.json(\n    52→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    53→        { status: 400 }\n    54→      );\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n    58→      ? platformPreference\n    59→      : \"telegram\";\n    60→\n    61→    const result = db\n    62→      .prepare(\n    63→        `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n    64→         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    65→      )\n    66→      .run(\n    67→        session.userId,\n    68→        bookTitle.trim(),\n    69→        bookAuthor.trim(),\n    70→        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n    71→        bookOlid || \"\",\n    72→        language || \"English\",\n    73→        readingPace.trim(),\n    74→        startDate,\n    75→        meetingFormat,\n    76→        size,\n    77→        requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n    78→        platform\n    79→      );\n    80→\n    81→    // Auto-join the author as a member\n    82→    db.prepare(\n    83→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    84→    ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n    85→\n    86→    return NextResponse.json(\n    87→      { id: result.lastInsertRowid },\n    88→      { status: 201 }\n    89→    );\n    90→  } catch (error) {\n    91→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    92→    return NextResponse.json(\n    93→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    94→      { status: 500 }\n    95→    );\n    96→  }\n    97→}\n    98→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"fdace1b0-3c69-4e80-b1b0-f0ef5025ab20","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:52.670Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a0167aa97356b7a54","tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":19169,"tool_uses":10,"duration_ms":7934},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"4a5f1fea-96ea-4bec-8bac-5121b2166365","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01WvkBSMws2dXy1ayVE9CaGd","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XWVxaGPUuuFg86HiD9msCT","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3361,"cache_read_input_tokens":15761,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3361,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"b85e3131-fa6a-4ab4-9a6a-ed08adab158f"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XWVxaGPUuuFg86HiD9msCT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    29→      { status: 400 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  // Check if already a member\n    38→  const existing = db\n    39→    .prepare(\n    40→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    41→    )\n    42→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    43→\n    44→  if (existing) {\n    45→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→  }\n    47→\n    48→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    49→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    50→    const { count } = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    53→\n    54→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    55→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    db.prepare(\n    59→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    60→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    61→\n    62→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    63→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    64→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    68→  });\n    69→\n    70→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    71→\n    72→  if (result.error) {\n    73→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    74→  }\n    75→\n    76→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    77→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    78→\n    79→  if (result.count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    80→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    81→  }\n    82→\n    83→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n    84→}\n    85→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"8bb672dc-191a-45c6-9893-4a35a96672d1","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:52.845Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01WvkBSMws2dXy1ayVE9CaGd","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NszcbUu6yBw5XcFCtdmSCP","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3361,"cache_read_input_tokens":15761,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3361,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"d2d0f1ad-96cf-4520-8610-8f19ad3834b7"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NszcbUu6yBw5XcFCtdmSCP","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    29→      { status: 400 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  // Check if already a member\n    38→  const isMember = db\n    39→    .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    40→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    41→\n    42→  if (isMember) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  // Check if already applied (pending)\n    47→  const existingApp = db\n    48→    .prepare(\n    49→      \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    50→    )\n    51→    .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    52→\n    53→  if (existingApp) {\n    54→    if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    55→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You have already applied\" }, { status: 400 });\n    56→    }\n    57→    if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    58→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already approved\" }, { status: 400 });\n    59→    }\n    60→    // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    61→    db.prepare(\n    62→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    63→    ).run(existingApp.id);\n    64→\n    65→    notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    66→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    67→  }\n    68→\n    69→  // Insert new application\n    70→  db.prepare(\n    71→    \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    72→  ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    73→\n    74→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    75→\n    76→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    77→}\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_014gwJmhsaHFsvNC4nXCKap9","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"0a768e5e-c368-44e2-95c7-18724e802bd9","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:01:53.047Z"}
