number;\n    39→  isMember: boolean;\n    40→  isAuthor: boolean;\n    41→  hasApplied: boolean;\n    42→  applicationStatus: string;\n    43→  pendingApplicants: Applicant[];\n    44→  currentUserId: number | null;\n    45→  telegramBotConfigured: boolean;\n    46→  discordBotConfigured: boolean;\n    47→  platform_preference: string;\n    48→  discord_link: string;\n    49→}\n    50→\n    51→export default function ListingDetailPage() {\n    52→  const { id } = useParams();\n    53→  const router = useRouter();\n    54→  const [listing, setListing] = useState<Listing | null>(null);\n    55→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    56→  const [joining, setJoining] = useState(false);\n    57→  const [applying, setApplying] = useState(false);\n    58→  const [decidingId, setDecidingId] = useState<number | null>(null);\n    59→  const [telegramLink, setTelegramLink] = useState(\"\");\n    60→  const [telegramSaving, setTelegramSaving] = useState(false);\n    61→  const [autoTelegramLoading, setAutoTelegramLoading] = useState(false);\n    62→  const [autoTelegramLink, setAutoTelegramLink] = useState(\"\");\n    63→  const [showManualFallback, setShowManualFallback] = useState(false);\n    64→  // Discord state\n    65→  const [discordLink, setDiscordLink] = useState(\"\");\n    66→  const [discordSaving, setDiscordSaving] = useState(false);\n    67→  const [autoDiscordLoading, setAutoDiscordLoading] = useState(false);\n    68→  const [autoDiscordLink, setAutoDiscordLink] = useState(\"\");\n    69→  const [showDiscordManualFallback, setShowDiscordManualFallback] = useState(false);\n    70→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    71→\n    72→  // Rating state\n    73→  const [givenRatings, setGivenRatings] = useState<Record<number, { score: number; comment: string }>>({});\n    74→  const [ratingScores, setRatingScores] = useState<Record<number, number>>({});\n    75→  const [ratingComments, setRatingComments] = useState<Record<number, string>>({});\n    76→  const [ratingSaving, setRatingSaving] = useState<number | null>(null);\n    77→  const [ratingMessage, setRatingMessage] = useState(\"\");\n    78→\n    79→  const [notAvailable, setNotAvailable] = useState(false);\n    80→\n    81→  const fetchListing = async () => {\n    82→    const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`);\n    83→    const data = await res.json();\n    84→    if (res.ok && data.listing) {\n    85→      setListing(data.listing);\n    86→      setTelegramLink(data.listing.telegram_link || \"\");\n    87→      setDiscordLink(data.listing.discord_link || \"\");\n    88→    } else if (res.status === 404) {\n    89→      setNotAvailable(true);\n    90→    }\n    91→    setLoading(false);\n    92→  };\n    93→\n    94→  useEffect(() => {\n    95→    fetchListing();\n    96→    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n    97→  }, [id]);\n    98→\n    99→  const fetchRatings = useCallback(async () => {\n   100→    if (!id) return;"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"19b9c7a9-5952-477a-95a8-8fb3d36957cc","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:02:26.170Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018AAmNMhAySgi9sbt9xd16i","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ReadingRow {\n     6→  id: number;\n     7→  book_title: string;\n     8→  book_author: string;\n     9→  book_cover_url: string;\n    10→  reading_pace: string;\n    11→  start_date: string;\n    12→  meeting_format: string;\n    13→  is_full: number;\n    14→  telegram_link: string;\n    15→  member_count: number;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  author_name: string;\n    18→  joined_at: string;\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→export async function GET() {\n    22→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    23→  if (!session) {\n    24→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    25→  }\n    26→\n    27→  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n    28→  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    29→    SELECT\n    30→      l.id,\n    31→      l.book_title,\n    32→      l.book_author,\n    33→      l.book_cover_url,\n    34→      l.reading_pace,\n    35→      l.start_date,\n    36→      l.meeting_format,\n    37→      l.is_full,\n    38→      l.telegram_link,\n    39→      l.max_group_size,\n    40→      u.display_name as author_name,\n    41→      lm.joined_at,\n    42→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    43→    FROM listing_members lm\n    44→    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    45→    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    46→    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    47→    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n    48→  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n    49→\n    50→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    51→\n    52→  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n    53→  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    54→    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n    58→  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n    59→  const history = readings.filter(\n    60→    (r) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today\n    61→  );\n    62→\n    63→  const upcoming = readings.filter(\n    64→    (r) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today\n    65→  );\n    66→\n    67→  const active = readings.filter(\n    68→    (r) => !history.includes(r) && !upcoming.includes(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n    69→  );\n    70→\n    71→  return NextResponse.json({\n    72→    active,\n    73→    history,\n    74→    upcoming,\n    75→    total: readings.length,\n    76→  });\n    77→}\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"53c144b8-eccd-4868-b0e0-6a13546031b9","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:02:26.640Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019eNW38BvDj2f1eSQc5dpLQ","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full review.\n\n---\n\n## Bookmate Codebase Review\n\nReviewing all API routes, auth, database layer, notification system, and frontend components.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Listing author can join their own group, corrupting member count logic\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 48-68\n\nThe `/join` endpoint adds the caller as a listing member but never checks whether the caller is the listing's author. The author is already auto-joined as a member at listing creation time (in `/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`, line 82-84). If an author hits the join endpoint a second time, the `INSERT` will fail with a SQLite unique constraint violation, which is caught by the outer `try`-less transaction and bubbles up as an unhandled exception (a 500 error) rather than the clear \"already a member\" message — because the pre-transaction membership check passes on the first call. More critically, if for any reason the author was not in `listing_members` yet (e.g., data inconsistency), they would be added a second time, making the `max_group_size` computation incorrect.\n\nThe fix is a simple guard identical to the `apply` route:\n```ts\nif (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. `parseInt(id)` result is never validated for NaN across all `[id]` routes\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, line 16\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, line 16\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, line 16\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts`, line 15\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/discord/route.ts`, line 15\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram/route.ts`, line 33\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-discord/route.ts`, line 26\n\nEvery dynamic route does `parseInt(id, 10)` and passes the result directly to SQLite without checking for `NaN`. `parseInt(\"abc\", 10)` returns `NaN`. `better-sqlite3` will coerce `NaN` to `NULL` in the SQL query (`WHERE id = NULL`), which matches zero rows. The result is a 404, not a crash, but it means the validation path is broken — the route silently treats any non-numeric ID as \"listing not found\" rather than returning a 400. This is also a latent bug if SQLite behavior ever changes or if the value is used in arithmetic elsewhere.\n\nThe consistent fix for all these routes:\n```ts\nconst listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\nif (isNaN(listingId)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\nNote that `/api/listings/create/route.ts` (line 50) and `/api/profile/reputation/route.ts` (line 24) already do `isNaN` checks for other integers — this validation is simply missing from the path parameter parsing.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Telegram and Discord webhook endpoints open by default — no secret configured means fully unauthenticated\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 26-31\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 23-28\n\nBoth webhook handlers verify the secret only `if (webhookSecret)`. If the `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` or `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET` environment variable is not set, the check is skipped entirely and any unauthenticated caller can POST arbitrary payloads. The Discord webhook handler processes a `type: \"link\"` action that writes to the database (updates `discord_link`, `discord_channel_id`) and sends notifications to all listing members. The Telegram handler writes `telegram_link` to listings and sends in-app notifications as well.\n\nAn attacker with knowledge of the endpoint can overwrite Telegram/Discord invite links for any listing with a malicious URL, then trigger notifications to all members pointing them to that URL.\n\nThe guard should fail closed, not open:\n```ts\nconst webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\nif (!webhookSecret) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n}\nconst headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\nif (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. `startDate` field is stored without format validation, enabling arbitrary string insertion\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`, line 74\n\n`startDate` is accepted from the request body and inserted directly into the database with no format check. The listings browse endpoint uses it in a date comparison (`l.start_date >= ?`, line 32) and the profile reading route does string comparison (`r.start_date <= today`, line 54) assuming ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` format. A caller can pass `\"'; DROP TABLE listings; --\"` — safe because parameterized queries are used — but can also pass arbitrarily long strings or values like `\"9999-99-99\"` that corrupt sort/filter results. The `meetingFormat` and `maxGroupSize` fields are validated; `startDate` is not.\n\nAdd:\n```ts\nif (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\nNote: the browse GET route at line 31 already applies this exact regex to the filter parameter — it is simply absent from the create route.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 5. `notifyGroupFull` in the join route can double-fire if called concurrently\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 79-81\n\nAfter the transaction completes, the code checks `if (result.count >= listing.max_group_size`. Because `listing` was fetched before the transaction, and because `notifyGroupFull` is called outside the transaction, if two users join near-simultaneously and both transactions succeed (one reaching exactly `max_group_size` and one triggering the pre-transaction `is_full = 0` check before the other's commit), both callers could satisfy the outer `if` condition. More concretely: because `listing.max_group_size` is a stale snapshot, `result.count >= listing.max_group_size` can be true for more than one concurrent request, sending duplicate group-full notifications to every member.\n\nThe guard should check `result.count === listing.max_group_size` (equality) rather than `>=`, which ensures only the exact last joiner triggers the notification:\n```ts\nif (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n  notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. `GET /api/ratings` does not validate that the requester is a member of the requested listing\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 137-188\n\nThe `GET` handler returns all ratings given and received by the current user for a listing, but never checks whether the current user is actually a member of that listing. Any authenticated user can query `GET /api/ratings?listingId=X` for any listing and retrieve the score and comment of ratings that user gave or received in that group, even if they have no relationship to it. While this leaks only the caller's own data (not other users' data), it breaks the stated intent that ratings are only available to group participants, and could be used to enumerate ratings across listings.\n\nAdd a membership check before the rating queries:\n```ts\nconst isMember = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n  .get(listingId, session.userId);\nif (!isMember) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are not a member of this group\" }, { status: 403 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. No database indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`\n\nSQLite does not automatically create indexes on foreign key columns. The following columns are queried repeatedly across multiple hot paths but have no indexes:\n\n- `listing_members.listing_id` — queried in every join, application approval, member count, and notification fan-out\n- `listing_members.user_id` — queried in membership checks for every protected endpoint\n- `notifications.user_id` — queried on every page load via the Navbar polling `/api/notifications`\n- `ratings.rated_user_id` — queried in the reputation endpoint\n\nWith SQLite, full-table scans on these columns will degrade noticeably once `listing_members` and `notifications` grow. The `PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id)` on `listing_members` covers lookups by `listing_id` only when it is the leftmost column; a reverse lookup by `user_id` still scans.\n\nAdd to `db.ts`:\n```sql\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | Issue | File |\n|---|----------|-------|------|\n| 1 | Critical | Author can re-join their own listing, causing 500 or count corruption | `listings/[id]/join/route.ts` |\n| 2 | Critical | `parseInt(id)` NaN never checked in any `[id]` route | All `listings/[id]/*/route.ts` |\n| 3 | Critical | Webhook auth skipped when secret env var is unset — unauthenticated DB writes possible | `telegram/webhook`, `discord/webhook` |\n| 4 | Important | `startDate` stored without format validation, corrupts sort/filter | `listings/create/route.ts` |\n| 5 | Important | Group-full notification can double-fire under concurrency | `listings/[id]/join/route.ts` |\n| 6 | Important | `GET /api/ratings` accessible to non-members of a listing | `ratings/route.ts` |\n| 7 | Important | No indexes on high-traffic FK columns | `lib/db.ts` |\n\nNo SQL injection vulnerabilities were found — all queries correctly use parameterized statements. The `iron-session` auth implementation is solid. The transaction usage in join and approval flows is correct in structure. The issues above are the genuine bugs and security gaps."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a40b2efc5e4496fbb (use SendMessage with to: 'a40b2efc5e4496fbb' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 49665\ntool_uses: 41\nduration_ms: 117329</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"fafdf91b-1e7b-45d9-897f-d7a4d8641f29","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:03:39.657Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js application in /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, code quality issues, and adherence to project conventions. \n\nFocus on:\n1. API routes in app/app/api/ - check for SQL injection, auth bypasses, missing validation\n2. Auth implementation in app/lib/session.ts and API routes\n3. Database operations in app/lib/db.ts - schema issues, missing indexes\n4. Notification system in app/lib/notifications.ts\n5. Frontend components - error handling, loading states\n6. Any race conditions or data integrity issues\n\nThe project uses: Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, SQLite via better-sqlite3, iron-session, bcryptjs.\n\nOnly report HIGH confidence issues that truly matter - bugs, security problems, or significant logic errors. Skip style nitpicks.","agentId":"a40b2efc5e4496fbb","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full review.\n\n---\n\n## Bookmate Codebase Review\n\nReviewing all API routes, auth, database layer, notification system, and frontend components.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Listing author can join their own group, corrupting member count logic\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 48-68\n\nThe `/join` endpoint adds the caller as a listing member but never checks whether the caller is the listing's author. The author is already auto-joined as a member at listing creation time (in `/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`, line 82-84). If an author hits the join endpoint a second time, the `INSERT` will fail with a SQLite unique constraint violation, which is caught by the outer `try`-less transaction and bubbles up as an unhandled exception (a 500 error) rather than the clear \"already a member\" message — because the pre-transaction membership check passes on the first call. More critically, if for any reason the author was not in `listing_members` yet (e.g., data inconsistency), they would be added a second time, making the `max_group_size` computation incorrect.\n\nThe fix is a simple guard identical to the `apply` route:\n```ts\nif (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. `parseInt(id)` result is never validated for NaN across all `[id]` routes\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, line 16\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, line 16\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, line 16\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts`, line 15\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/discord/route.ts`, line 15\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram/route.ts`, line 33\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/auto-discord/route.ts`, line 26\n\nEvery dynamic route does `parseInt(id, 10)` and passes the result directly to SQLite without checking for `NaN`. `parseInt(\"abc\", 10)` returns `NaN`. `better-sqlite3` will coerce `NaN` to `NULL` in the SQL query (`WHERE id = NULL`), which matches zero rows. The result is a 404, not a crash, but it means the validation path is broken — the route silently treats any non-numeric ID as \"listing not found\" rather than returning a 400. This is also a latent bug if SQLite behavior ever changes or if the value is used in arithmetic elsewhere.\n\nThe consistent fix for all these routes:\n```ts\nconst listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\nif (isNaN(listingId)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\nNote that `/api/listings/create/route.ts` (line 50) and `/api/profile/reputation/route.ts` (line 24) already do `isNaN` checks for other integers — this validation is simply missing from the path parameter parsing.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Telegram and Discord webhook endpoints open by default — no secret configured means fully unauthenticated\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 26-31\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 23-28\n\nBoth webhook handlers verify the secret only `if (webhookSecret)`. If the `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` or `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET` environment variable is not set, the check is skipped entirely and any unauthenticated caller can POST arbitrary payloads. The Discord webhook handler processes a `type: \"link\"` action that writes to the database (updates `discord_link`, `discord_channel_id`) and sends notifications to all listing members. The Telegram handler writes `telegram_link` to listings and sends in-app notifications as well.\n\nAn attacker with knowledge of the endpoint can overwrite Telegram/Discord invite links for any listing with a malicious URL, then trigger notifications to all members pointing them to that URL.\n\nThe guard should fail closed, not open:\n```ts\nconst webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\nif (!webhookSecret) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n}\nconst headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\nif (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. `startDate` field is stored without format validation, enabling arbitrary string insertion\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts`, line 74\n\n`startDate` is accepted from the request body and inserted directly into the database with no format check. The listings browse endpoint uses it in a date comparison (`l.start_date >= ?`, line 32) and the profile reading route does string comparison (`r.start_date <= today`, line 54) assuming ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` format. A caller can pass `\"'; DROP TABLE listings; --\"` — safe because parameterized queries are used — but can also pass arbitrarily long strings or values like `\"9999-99-99\"` that corrupt sort/filter results. The `meetingFormat` and `maxGroupSize` fields are validated; `startDate` is not.\n\nAdd:\n```ts\nif (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\nNote: the browse GET route at line 31 already applies this exact regex to the filter parameter — it is simply absent from the create route.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 5. `notifyGroupFull` in the join route can double-fire if called concurrently\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 79-81\n\nAfter the transaction completes, the code checks `if (result.count >= listing.max_group_size`. Because `listing` was fetched before the transaction, and because `notifyGroupFull` is called outside the transaction, if two users join near-simultaneously and both transactions succeed (one reaching exactly `max_group_size` and one triggering the pre-transaction `is_full = 0` check before the other's commit), both callers could satisfy the outer `if` condition. More concretely: because `listing.max_group_size` is a stale snapshot, `result.count >= listing.max_group_size` can be true for more than one concurrent request, sending duplicate group-full notifications to every member.\n\nThe guard should check `result.count === listing.max_group_size` (equality) rather than `>=`, which ensures only the exact last joiner triggers the notification:\n```ts\nif (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n  notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. `GET /api/ratings` does not validate that the requester is a member of the requested listing\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 137-188\n\nThe `GET` handler returns all ratings given and received by the current user for a listing, but never checks whether the current user is actually a member of that listing. Any authenticated user can query `GET /api/ratings?listingId=X` for any listing and retrieve the score and comment of ratings that user gave or received in that group, even if they have no relationship to it. While this leaks only the caller's own data (not other users' data), it breaks the stated intent that ratings are only available to group participants, and could be used to enumerate ratings across listings.\n\nAdd a membership check before the rating queries:\n```ts\nconst isMember = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n  .get(listingId, session.userId);\nif (!isMember) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are not a member of this group\" }, { status: 403 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. No database indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`\n\nSQLite does not automatically create indexes on foreign key columns. The following columns are queried repeatedly across multiple hot paths but have no indexes:\n\n- `listing_members.listing_id` — queried in every join, application approval, member count, and notification fan-out\n- `listing_members.user_id` — queried in membership checks for every protected endpoint\n- `notifications.user_id` — queried on every page load via the Navbar polling `/api/notifications`\n- `ratings.rated_user_id` — queried in the reputation endpoint\n\nWith SQLite, full-table scans on these columns will degrade noticeably once `listing_members` and `notifications` grow. The `PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id)` on `listing_members` covers lookups by `listing_id` only when it is the leftmost column; a reverse lookup by `user_id` still scans.\n\nAdd to `db.ts`:\n```sql\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | Issue | File |\n|---|----------|-------|------|\n| 1 | Critical | Author can re-join their own listing, causing 500 or count corruption | `listings/[id]/join/route.ts` |\n| 2 | Critical | `parseInt(id)` NaN never checked in any `[id]` route | All `listings/[id]/*/route.ts` |\n| 3 | Critical | Webhook auth skipped when secret env var is unset — unauthenticated DB writes possible | `telegram/webhook`, `discord/webhook` |\n| 4 | Important | `startDate` stored without format validation, corrupts sort/filter | `listings/create/route.ts` |\n| 5 | Important | Group-full notification can double-fire under concurrency | `listings/[id]/join/route.ts` |\n| 6 | Important | `GET /api/ratings` accessible to non-members of a listing | `ratings/route.ts` |\n| 7 | Important | No indexes on high-traffic FK columns | `lib/db.ts` |\n\nNo SQL injection vulnerabilities were found — all queries correctly use parameterized statements. The `iron-session` auth implementation is solid. The transaction usage in join and approval flows is correct in structure. The issues above are the genuine bugs and security gaps."}],"totalDurationMs":117329,"totalTokens":49665,"totalToolUseCount":41,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":381,"cache_read_input_tokens":46432,"output_tokens":2851,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":381},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KpHa8QC7HPGCEmZnsACDHE","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    29→      { status: 400 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  // Check if already a member\n    38→  const existing = db\n    39→    .prepare(\n    40→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    41→    )\n    42→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    43→\n    44→  if (existing) {\n    45→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→  }\n    47→\n    48→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    49→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    50→    const { count } = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    53→\n    54→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    55→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    db.prepare(\n    59→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    60→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    61→\n    62→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    63→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    64→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    68→  });\n    69→\n    70→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    71→\n    72→  if (result.error) {\n    73→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    74→  }\n    75→\n    76→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    77→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    78→\n    79→  if (result.count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    80→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    81→  }\n    82→\n    83→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n    84→}\n    85→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"156fa2a7-e8ac-4505-84d7-d3040b3f90fe","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:03:49.266Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function POST(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Check if already a member\n  const existing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  if (existing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    const { count } = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    }\n\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n\n    const newCount = count + 1;\n    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    }\n\n    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n  });\n\n  const result = joinTransaction();\n\n  if (result.error) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\n  if (result.count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n}\n","numLines":85,"startLine":1,"totalLines":85}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":3,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"fil"}},"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"7809579e-83b2-4d22-828e-2f83cd4725ea"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Tr4fgEaEseMBoVoGHfqLRj","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createChatInviteLink,\n     5→  exportChatInviteLink,\n     6→  sendMessage,\n     7→  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n     8→} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     9→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    10→\n    11→/**\n    12→ * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n    13→ *\n    14→ * Handles two types of updates:\n    15→ *\n    16→ * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n    17→ *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n    18→ *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n    19→ *    and save it to the listing.\n    20→ *\n    21→ * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n    22→ *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n    23→ */\n    24→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    25→  // Verify webhook secret if configured\n    26→  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    27→  if (webhookSecret) {\n    28→    const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n    29→    if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    30→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→    }\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n    35→  try {\n    36→    update = await req.json();\n    37→  } catch {\n    38→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n    42→  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    43→    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n    44→      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    45→    );\n    46→  }\n    47→\n    48→  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n    49→  if (update.message) {\n    50→    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n    54→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→interface ChatMemberUpdate {\n    58→  chat: {\n    59→    id: number;\n    60→    title?: string;\n    61→    type: string;\n    62→  };\n    63→  from: {\n    64→    id: number;\n    65→    first_name: string;\n    66→  };\n    67→  new_chat_member: {\n    68→    user: {\n    69→      id: number;\n    70→      is_bot: boolean;\n    71→    };\n    72→    status: string;\n    73→  };\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→interface TelegramMessage {\n    77→  chat: {\n    78→    id: number;\n    79→    type: string;\n    80→    title?: string;\n    81→  };\n    82→  text?: string;\n    83→  from?: {\n    84→    id: number;\n    85→  };\n    86→}\n    87→\n    88→/**\n    89→ * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n    90→ *\n    91→ * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n    92→ * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n    93→ * 1. Creates the group\n    94→ * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n    95→ * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n    96→ *\n    97→ * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n    98→ */\n    99→async function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n   100→  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n   101→\n   102→  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n   103→  if (\n   104→    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n   105→    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n   106→  ) {\n   107→    return;\n   108→  }\n   109→\n   110→  // Only process group chats\n   111→  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n   112→    return;\n   113→  }\n   114→\n   115→  // Try to find the listing this group belongs to.\n   116→  // Strategy 1: Check pending_telegram_groups mapping table\n   117→  const pending = db\n   118→    .prepare(\n   119→      \"SELECT listing_id FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\"\n   120→    )\n   121→    .get(chat.id) as { listing_id: number } | undefined;\n   122→\n   123→  if (pending) {\n   124→    await linkTelegramToListing(pending.listing_id, chat.id);\n   125→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\").run(\n   126→      chat.id\n   127→    );\n   128→    return;\n   129→  }\n   130→\n   131→  // Strategy 2: Check for listings awaiting auto-telegram (most recent unlinked full listing by this author)\n   132→  // We store the chat ID and wait for the /start command or title match\n   133→  // For now, store the mapping and send a welcome message\n   134→  await sendMessage(\n   135→    chat.id,\n   136→    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n   137→      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n   138→      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n   139→  );\n   140→\n   141→  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n   142→  try {\n   143→    db.prepare(\n   144→      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n   145→    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n   146→  } catch {\n   147→    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n   148→  }\n   149→}\n   150→\n   151→/**\n   152→ * Handle messages in group chats.\n   153→ * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n   154→ */\n   155→async function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n   156→  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n   157→    return;\n   158→  }\n   159→\n   160→  const text = message.text.trim();\n   161→\n   162→  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n   163→  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n   164→    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n   165→    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n   166→    if (listingId) {\n   167→      await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   168→      return;\n   169→    }\n   170→  }\n   171→\n   172→  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n   173→  if (text === \"/link\") {\n   174→    // Find the most recent full listing without a telegram link\n   175→    // that was authored by someone (we can't verify Telegram user = Bookmate user\n   176→    // without account linking, so we just report available listings)\n   177→    const unlinkedListings = db\n   178→      .prepare(\n   179→        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n   180→         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n   181→         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n   182→         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n   183→      )\n   184→      .all() as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n   185→\n   186→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n   187→      await sendMessage(\n   188→        message.chat.id,\n   189→        \"No unlinked reading groups found. The group may already be set up!\"\n   190→      );\n   191→      return;\n   192→    }\n   193→\n   194→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n   195→      // Auto-link the only available listing\n   196→      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n   197→      return;\n   198→    }\n   199→\n   200→    // Show options\n   201→    const listText = unlinkedListings\n   202→      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${l.book_title}\"`)\n   203→      .join(\"\\n\");\n   204→    await sendMessage(\n   205→      message.chat.id,\n   206→      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n   207→    );\n   208→    return;\n   209→  }\n   210→\n   211→  // Handle /link_ID command\n   212→  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n   213→  if (linkMatch) {\n   214→    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n   215→    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   216→  }\n   217→}\n   218→\n   219→/**\n   220→ * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n   221→ * 1. Generate an invite link\n   222→ * 2. Save it to the listing\n   223→ * 3. Notify all members\n   224→ * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n   225→ */\n   226→async function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n   227→  const listing = db\n   228→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   229→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   230→\n   231→  if (!listing) {\n   232→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n   233→    return;\n   234→  }\n   235→\n   236→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n   237→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n   238→    return;\n   239→  }\n   240→\n   241→  // Try to create an invite link\n   242→  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   243→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   244→    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n   245→    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   246→  }\n   247→\n   248→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   249→    await sendMessage(\n   250→      chatId,\n   251→      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n   252→        \"then try again with /link\"\n   253→    );\n   254→    return;\n   255→  }\n   256→\n   257→  // Save to database\n   258→  db.prepare(\n   259→    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   260→  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n   261→\n   262→  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n   263→  const members = db\n   264→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   265→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   266→\n   267→  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n   268→  for (const member of members) {\n   269→    createNotification(\n   270→      member.user_id,\n   271→      listingId,\n   272→      \"telegram_ready\",\n   273→      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n   274→    );\n   275→  }\n   276→\n   277→  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n   278→  await sendMessage(\n   279→    chatId,\n   280→    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${bookTitle}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n   281→      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n   282→      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n   283→  );\n   284→}\n   285→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"3ce96cb4-dfb3-4692-aefe-c85f60281034","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:03:49.718Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport {\n  createChatInviteLink,\n  exportChatInviteLink,\n  sendMessage,\n  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\nimport { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\n/**\n * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n *\n * Handles two types of updates:\n *\n * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n *    and save it to the listing.\n *\n * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  // Verify webhook secret if configured\n  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  if (webhookSecret) {\n    const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n    if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    }\n  }\n\n  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n  try {\n    update = await req.json();\n  } catch {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n  if (update.message) {\n    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n  }\n\n  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n\ninterface ChatMemberUpdate {\n  chat: {\n    id: number;\n    title?: string;\n    type: string;\n  };\n  from: {\n    id: number;\n    first_name: string;\n  };\n  new_chat_member: {\n    user: {\n      id: number;\n      is_bot: boolean;\n    };\n    status: string;\n  };\n}\n\ninterface TelegramMessage {\n  chat: {\n    id: number;\n    type: string;\n    title?: string;\n  };\n  text?: string;\n  from?: {\n    id: number;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n *\n * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n * 1. Creates the group\n * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n *\n * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n */\nasync function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n\n  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n  if (\n    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n  ) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Only process group chats\n  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Try to find the listing this group belongs to.\n  // Strategy 1: Check pending_telegram_groups mapping table\n  const pending = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT listing_id FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(chat.id) as { listing_id: number } | undefined;\n\n  if (pending) {\n    await linkTelegramToListing(pending.listing_id, chat.id);\n    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\").run(\n      chat.id\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Strategy 2: Check for listings awaiting auto-telegram (most recent unlinked full listing by this author)\n  // We store the chat ID and wait for the /start command or title match\n  // For now, store the mapping and send a welcome message\n  await sendMessage(\n    chat.id,\n    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n  );\n\n  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n  try {\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n  } catch {\n    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Handle messages in group chats.\n * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n */\nasync function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const text = message.text.trim();\n\n  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n    if (listingId) {\n      await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n  if (text === \"/link\") {\n    // Find the most recent full listing without a telegram link\n    // that was authored by someone (we can't verify Telegram user = Bookmate user\n    // without account linking, so we just report available listings)\n    const unlinkedListings = db\n      .prepare(\n        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n      )\n      .all() as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n\n    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"No unlinked reading groups found. The group may already be set up!\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n      // Auto-link the only available listing\n      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Show options\n    const listText = unlinkedListings\n      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${l.book_title}\"`)\n      .join(\"\\n\");\n    await sendMessage(\n      message.chat.id,\n      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Handle /link_ID command\n  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n  if (linkMatch) {\n    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n * 1. Generate an invite link\n * 2. Save it to the listing\n * 3. Notify all members\n * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n */\nasync function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Try to create an invite link\n  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n  }\n\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    await sendMessage(\n      chatId,\n      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n        \"then try again with /link\"\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Save to database\n  db.prepare(\n    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n\n  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n  for (const member of members) {\n    createNotification(\n      member.user_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"telegram_ready\",\n      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n  await sendMessage(\n    chatId,\n    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${bookTitle}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n  );\n}\n","numLines":285,"startLine":1,"totalLines":285}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NCdK8E2FaSsDzEa7tbNynC","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createTextChannel,\n     5→  createChannelInvite,\n     6→  sendChannelMessage,\n     7→} from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     8→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     9→\n    10→/**\n    11→ * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n    12→ *\n    13→ * This endpoint handles:\n    14→ * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n    15→ *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n    16→ *\n    17→ * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n    18→ * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n    19→ * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n    20→ */\n    21→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    22→  // Verify webhook secret if configured\n    23→  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    24→  if (webhookSecret) {\n    25→    const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n    26→    if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    27→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    28→    }\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  try {\n    32→    const body = await req.json();\n    33→    const { type } = body;\n    34→\n    35→    if (type === \"link\") {\n    36→      return handleLink(body);\n    37→    }\n    38→\n    39→    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    40→    if (type === 1) {\n    41→      // PING - respond with PONG\n    42→      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    43→    }\n    44→\n    45→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→  } catch (err) {\n    47→    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    48→    return NextResponse.json(\n    49→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    50→      { status: 500 }\n    51→    );\n    52→  }\n    53→}\n    54→\n    55→async function handleLink(body: {\n    56→  guildId: string;\n    57→  listingId: number;\n    58→  channelId?: string;\n    59→}) {\n    60→  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n    61→\n    62→  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    63→    return NextResponse.json(\n    64→      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n    65→      { status: 400 }\n    66→    );\n    67→  }\n    68→\n    69→  const listing = db\n    70→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    71→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    72→\n    73→  if (!listing) {\n    74→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    75→  }\n    76→\n    77→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    78→    return NextResponse.json(\n    79→      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n    80→      { status: 400 }\n    81→    );\n    82→  }\n    83→\n    84→  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n    85→\n    86→  // If no channel specified, create one\n    87→  if (!targetChannelId) {\n    88→    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n    89→    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n    90→      .toLowerCase()\n    91→      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n    92→      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n    93→\n    94→    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n    95→      guildId,\n    96→      channelName,\n    97→      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n    98→    );\n    99→\n   100→    if (!channel) {\n   101→      return NextResponse.json(\n   102→        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n   103→        { status: 500 }\n   104→      );\n   105→    }\n   106→\n   107→    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n   108→  }\n   109→\n   110→  // Create invite link\n   111→  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n   112→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   113→    return NextResponse.json(\n   114→      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n   115→      { status: 500 }\n   116→    );\n   117→  }\n   118→\n   119→  // Save to DB\n   120→  db.prepare(\n   121→    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   122→  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n   123→\n   124→  // Notify all members\n   125→  const members = db\n   126→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   127→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   128→\n   129→  for (const member of members) {\n   130→    createNotification(\n   131→      member.user_id,\n   132→      listingId,\n   133→      \"discord_ready\",\n   134→      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n   135→    );\n   136→  }\n   137→\n   138→  // Send welcome message to the channel\n   139→  await sendChannelMessage(\n   140→    targetChannelId,\n   141→    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n   142→      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n   143→      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n   144→      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n   145→      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n   146→  );\n   147→\n   148→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n   149→}\n   150→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"357d246c-ee41-4386-93c6-4c72f089e3af","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:03:50.172Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport {\n  createTextChannel,\n  createChannelInvite,\n  sendChannelMessage,\n} from \"@/lib/discord\";\nimport { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\n/**\n * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n *\n * This endpoint handles:\n * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n *\n * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  // Verify webhook secret if configured\n  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  if (webhookSecret) {\n    const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n    if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    }\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const { type } = body;\n\n    if (type === \"link\") {\n      return handleLink(body);\n    }\n\n    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    if (type === 1) {\n      // PING - respond with PONG\n      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    }\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nasync function handleLink(body: {\n  guildId: string;\n  listingId: number;\n  channelId?: string;\n}) {\n  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n\n  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n\n  // If no channel specified, create one\n  if (!targetChannelId) {\n    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n      .toLowerCase()\n      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n\n    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n      guildId,\n      channelName,\n      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n    );\n\n    if (!channel) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n        { status: 500 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n  }\n\n  // Create invite link\n  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Save to DB\n  db.prepare(\n    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n\n  // Notify all members\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  for (const member of members) {\n    createNotification(\n      member.user_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"discord_ready\",\n      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Send welcome message to the channel\n  await sendChannelMessage(\n    targetChannelId,\n    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n}\n","numLines":150,"startLine":1,"totalLines":150}}}

31→      );\n    32→    }\n    33→\n    34→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    35→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    36→      return NextResponse.json(\n    37→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    38→        { status: 400 }\n    39→      );\n    40→    }\n    41→\n    42→    if (![\"voice\",  65→      )\n    66→      .run(\n    67→        session.userId,\n    68→        bookTitle.trim(),\n    69→        bookAuthor.trim(),\n    70→        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n    71→        bookOlid || 98→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"54e97735-0d8d-4948-b749-f29a7eebe980","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:03:51.030Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const {\n      bookTitle,\n      bookAuthor,\n      bookCoverUrl,\n      bookOlid,\n      language,\n      readingPace,\n      startDate,\n      meetingFormat,\n      maxGroupSize,\n      requiresApproval,\n      platformPreference,\n    } = body;\n\n    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n      ? platformPreference\n      : \"telegram\";\n\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n      )\n      .run(\n        session.userId,\n        bookTitle.trim(),\n        bookAuthor.trim(),\n        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n        bookOlid || \"\",\n        language || \"English\",\n        readingPace.trim(),\n        startDate,\n        meetingFormat,\n        size,\n        requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n        platform\n      );\n\n    // Auto-join the author as a member\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { id: result.lastInsertRowid },\n      { status: 201 }\n    );\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n","numLines":98,"startLine":1,"totalLines":98}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019Wk4xzdQ7HZKw3JBQpgWFT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * POST /api/ratings — Submit a rating for a group member\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Body: { listingId, ratedUserId, score (1-5), comment? }\n    10→ *\n    11→ * Rules:\n    12→ * - Both rater and rated must be members of the listing\n    13→ * - The listing must be full (group completed)\n    14→ * - Cannot rate yourself\n    15→ * - One rating per rater-rated pair per listing (can update)\n    16→ */\n    17→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    18→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    19→  if (!session) {\n    20→    return NextResponse.json(\n    21→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    22→      { status: 401 }\n    23→    );\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  const body = await req.json();\n    27→  const { listingId, ratedUserId, score, comment } = body;\n    28→\n    29→  if (!listingId || !ratedUserId || !score) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"listingId, ratedUserId, and score are required\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (score < 1 || score > 5 || !Number.isInteger(score)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Score must be an integer between 1 and  \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n   117→    ).run(listingId, session.userId, ratedUserId, score, comment || \"\");\n   118→\n in \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n   125→    );\n   126→  }\n   127→\n   128→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   129→}\n   130→\n   131→/**\n   132→ * GET /api/ratings?listingId=X — Get ratings for a listing\n   133→ *\n   134→ * Returns ratings the current user has given and received in a listing,\n   135→ * plus the members who can still be rated.\n   136→ */\n   137→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n   138→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   139→  if (!session) {\n   140→    return NextResponse.json(\n   141→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n   142→ 149→      { error: \"listingId parameter required\" },\n   150→      { status: 400 }\n   151→    );\n   152→  }\n   153→\n   154→  // Ratings the current user has given in this listing\n   155→  const givenRatings = db\n   156→    .prepare(\n   157→      `SELECT r.rated_user_id, r.score, r.comment, u.display_name\n   158→       FROM ratings r\n   159→       JOIN users u ON r.rated_user_id = u.id\n   160→       WHERE r.listing_id = ? AND r.rater_id = ?`\n   161→    )\n   162→    .all(listingId, session.userId) as {\n   163→      rated_user_id: number;\n   164→      score: number;\n   165→      comment: string;\n   166→      display_name: string;\n   167→    }[];\n   168→\n   169→  // Ratings the current user has received in this listing\n   170→  const receivedRatings = db\n   171→    .prepare(\n   172→      `SELECT r.rater_id, r.score, r.comment, u.display_name\n   173→       FROM ratings r\n   174→       JOIN users u ON r.rater_id = u.id\n   175→       WHERE r.listing_id = ? AND r.rated_user_id = ?`\n   176→    )\n   177→    .all(listingId, session.userId) as {\n   178→      rater_id: number;\n   179→      score: number;\n   180→      comment: string;\n   181→      display_name: string;\n   182→    }[];\n   183→\n   184→  return NextResponse.json({\n   185→    givenRatings,\n   186→    receivedRatings,\n   187→  });\n   188→}\n   189→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcf24925-f3c9-4a21-8262-ea37e50a9e93","uuid":"54fb602b-f782-4459-8cae-17bac8f774e7","timestamp":"2026-03-20T13:03:51.104Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\n/**\n * POST /api/ratings — Submit a rating for a group member\n *\n * Body: { listingId, ratedUserId, score (1-5), comment? }\n *\n * Rules:\n * - Both rater and rated must be members of the listing\n * - The listing must be full (group completed)\n * - Cannot rate yourself\n * - One rating per rater-rated pair per listing (can update)\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const body = await req.json();\n  const { listingId, ratedUserId, score, comment } = body;\n\n  if (!listingId || !ratedUserId || !score) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"listingId, ratedUserId, and score are required\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (score < 1 || score > 5 || !Number.isInteger(score)) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Score must be an integer between 1 and 5\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (ratedUserId === session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You cannot rate yourself\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (comment && comment.length > 1000) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Comment must be at most 1000 characters\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Verify listing exists and is full\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT id, book_title, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { id: number; book_title: string; is_full: number } | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Listing not found\" },\n      { status: 404 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Ratings are only available for completed reading groups\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Verify both users are members\n  const raterMember = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  if (!raterMember) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You must be a member of this group to rate\" },\n      { status: 403 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const ratedMember = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, ratedUserId);\n\n  if (!ratedMember) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"The user you are rating must be a member of this group\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Upsert rating (update if exists)\n  const existing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT id FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ? AND rater_id = ? AND rated_user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId, ratedUserId) as { id: number } | undefined;\n\n  if (existing) {\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE ratings SET score = ?, comment = ?, created_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    ).run(score, comment || \"\", existing.id);\n  } else {\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, session.userId, ratedUserId, score, comment || \"\");\n\n    // Notify the rated user\n    createNotification(\n      ratedUserId,\n      listingId,\n      \"rating_received\",\n      `Someone rated your participation in \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n\n/**\n * GET /api/ratings?listingId=X — Get ratings for a listing\n *\n * Returns ratings the current user has given and received in a listing,\n * plus the members who can still be rated.\n */\nexport async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const listingId = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"listingId\");\n  if (!listingId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"listingId parameter required\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Ratings the current user has given in this listing\n  const givenRatings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT r.rated_user_id, r.score, r.comment, u.display_name\n       FROM ratings r\n       JOIN users u ON r.rated_user_id = u.id\n       WHERE r.listing_id = ? AND r.rater_id = ?`\n    )\n    .all(listingId, session.userId) as {\n      rated_user_id: number;\n      score: number;\n      comment: string;\n      display_name: string;\n    }[];\n\n  // Ratings the current user has received in this listing\n  const receivedRatings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT r.rater_id, r.score, r.comment, u.display_name\n       FROM ratings r\n       JOIN users u ON r.rater_id = u.id\n       WHERE r.listing_id = ? AND r.rated_user_id = ?`\n    )\n    .all(listingId, session.userId) as {\n      rater_id: number;\n      score: number;\n      comment: string;\n      display_name: string;\n    }[];\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    givenRatings,\n    receivedRatings,\n  });\n}\n","numLines":189,"startLine":1,"totalLines":189}}}

user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n
